zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Adams於1511年出生在英國賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯時，她的父親Thomas Rennelegh Adams56歲，母親Joan T Ewing32歲。她和John Adams至少有7個兒子和5個女兒。她去世於1578年在她的家鄉享年67歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特麥德斯同。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Adams于1511年出生在英国賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯时，她的父亲Thomas Rennelegh Adams56岁，母亲Joan T Ewing32岁。她和John Adams至少有7个儿子和5个女儿。她去世于1578年在她的家乡享年67岁，埋葬在英国英格兰肯特麥德斯同。

nameMeanings.name: Adams
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Dutch, and German (mainly northwestern Germany): patronymic from the personal name Adam . In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Greek Adamopoulos , Serbian and Croatian Adamović (see Adamovich ), Polish (and Jewish) Adamski .
Irish and Scottish: adopted for McAdam or a Scottish variant of Adam , with excrescent -s.

History: This surname was borne by two early presidents of the US, father and son. They were descended from Henry Adams, who settled in Braintree, MA, in 1635/6, from Barton St. David, Somerset, England. The younger of them, John Quincy Adams (1767–1848) derived his middle name from his maternal grandmother's surname (see   Quincy  ). — Another important New England family, established mainly in NH, is descended from William Adams, who emigrated from Shropshire, England, to Dedham, MA, in 1628. James Hopkins Adams (1812–61), governor of SC, was unconnected with either of these families, his ancestry being Welsh; his forebears entered North America through PA.

variants.name: Adam
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Umberto, Aldo, Giulio, Paolo, Primo, Silvio.
German: Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name Adam , from Adami, the Latin genitive. This surname is also found in France (Lorraine).
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name   Adamo  . This surname is also found in Corsica and southern France.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Adams was born in 1511, in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England, her father, Thomas Rennelegh Adams, was 56 and her mother, Joan T Ewing, was 32. She had at least 7 sons and 5 daughters with John Adams. She died in 1578, in her hometown, at the age of 67, and was buried in Maidstone, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Adams est née en 1511, à Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Rennelegh Adams, avait 56 ans et sa mère, Joan T Ewing, avait 32 ans. Elle a eu au moins 7 fils et 5 filles avec John Adams. Elle est décédée en 1578, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterrée à Maidstone, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Adams 1511 in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Rennelegh Adams 56 und ihre Mutter Joan T Ewing 32. Sie hatte mit John Adams mindestens 7 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb 1578 im Alter von 67 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Maidstone, Kent, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Adams è nata il nel 1511 nel Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England, sua padre, Thomas Rennelegh Adams, aveva 56 anni e sua madre, Joan T Ewing, aveva 32 anni. Lei avuto almeno 7 figli e 5 figlie con John Adams. Morì nel 1578, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolta a Maidstone, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Adamsが1511年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアサウブリジェウオルスで生まれた時，父Thomas Rennelegh Adamsは56歳，母Joan T Ewingは32歳であった。彼女はJohn Adamsとの間に少なくとも7人の息子と5人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1578年に故郷で67歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスケントメイドストーンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Adams(이)가 1511년 영국 허트퍼드실르 사w브리지워스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Rennelegh Adams(은)는 56세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan T Ewing(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 John Adams(와)과 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1578년 그녀의 고향에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 메이드스톤에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Adams nasceu em 1511, em Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Rennelegh Adams, tinha 56 anos e sua mãe, Joan T Ewing, tinha 32 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 7 filhos e 5 filhas com John Adams. Ela faleceu em 1578, em sua cidade natal, com 67 anos, e foi sepultada em Maidstone, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Adams родилась в 1511 г. в местности савбриджюорт, хиртфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Rennelegh Adams было 56 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan T Ewing, 32. У неё было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 5 дочерей от John Adams. Она умерла в 1578 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 67 лет и была похоронена в местности меидстоун, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Adams nació en 1511, en Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Rennelegh Adams, tenía 56 años y su mamá, Joan T Ewing, tenía 32 años. Tuvo por lo menos 7 hijos y 5 hijas con John Adams. Murió en 1578, en su pueblo, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultada en Maidstone, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
